context: In 1990, South Korea's music scene was dominated by sentimental ballads and trot, with no formal national singles chart yet. This was the pre-K-pop era — two years before Seo Taiji and Boys would revolutionize Korean popular music. The biggest stars were ballad singers like Byun Jin-sub and the newly debuted Shin Seung-hun, whose first album sold 1.4 million copies. Dance pop was emerging through Kim Wan-sun ('Korea's Madonna') and Kang Suji.

tracks.title: Geouldo An Boneun Yeoja (A Woman Who Doesn't Look in the Mirror)
Seulpeun Pyojeong Haji Marayo (Don't Look So Sad)
Bi Oneun Nal Suchaehwa (Watercolor on a Rainy Day)
Bogo Sipeun Eolgul (A Face I Miss)
Misoso Soge Bicchin Geudae (Reflection of You in Your Smile)
